title: narcissus papyraceus subsp. barlae
intro: Narcissus papyraceus subsp. barlae, also known as the Barlae's daffodil, is a subspecies of the Narcissus papyraceus plant, belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, including the presence of alkaloids such as barbare and lycorine, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Narcissus papyraceus subsp. barlae include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from the bulbs, which can be dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it is essential to consult a medical professional before using any herbal remedies.
